description: 56M chest pain, smoker, otherwise low risk — classic HEART case
transcript:
Dr. Patel: Hi sir, I'm Dr. Patel. What brings you in today?
Patient: I've been having some chest pain since this morning.
Dr. Patel: Can you tell me about it? When did it start?
Patient: About six hours ago. Came on while I was at my desk. Kind of a pressure in the middle of my chest.
Dr. Patel: Anything make it better or worse?
Patient: It comes and goes. It's not really worse with movement.
Dr. Patel: Any radiation to your arm, jaw, back?
Patient: Maybe a little to my left arm. Hard to tell.
Dr. Patel: Short of breath, sweaty?
Patient: A little sweaty when it first hit.
Dr. Patel: Any nausea?
Patient: A little.
Dr. Patel: How old are you?
Patient: Fifty-six.
Dr. Patel: Any medical problems?
Patient: I smoke. About a pack a day for thirty years.
Dr. Patel: Blood pressure, cholesterol, diabetes?
Patient: They told me my cholesterol was borderline last year. No diabetes.
Dr. Patel: Family history of heart disease?
Patient: My dad had a bypass at sixty-two.
Dr. Patel: Any prior heart issues for you?
Patient: No.
Dr. Patel to nurse: Let's get an EKG, troponin, CBC, BMP, repeat trop in three hours. Aspirin 324 chewed if no contraindications.

groundTruth.notes: Adult chest pain with ACS in differential. Surface HEART. Age and risk factors clearly heard. EKG and troponin pending — should appear in missing_elements.
